title: Senior Executives
name: Senior Executives
context_line: Experience  ·  15+ years        Context  ·  B2B / C-Suite        Difficulty  ·  Advanced-Strategic
tagline: A 200-item, multi-format, psychometrically structured assessment covering five pillars of professional growth.
about:
This 200-item assessment is designed for senior executives operating at C-suite, board, and other senior leadership levels. Items reflect the complexity of organisational strategy, capital allocation, executive communication, sustained performance over decades, and the technology governance expectations facing modern senior leaders.
Calibration vs. earlier-career assessments
Items here assume direct experience with senior-level work: board engagement, executive team leadership, public communication at scale, capital allocation decisions, crisis management, and the structural challenges of sustained high performance over years. Distractors are designed to be plausible to senior leaders - including common patterns that look reasonable but are sub-optimal.
how_to: Answer honestly. Self-concept items have no 'right' answer - they measure your perception of yourself. Situational judgment items present scenarios where multiple options reflect things experienced senior leaders actually do; the highest-scoring answer reflects the response with strongest evidence base or strategic merit. Time required: 75-90 minutes.
scoring_steps: STEP 1  Compute the raw score for each pillar by summing item-level scores using the Answer Key. Knowledge items contribute 0–1; SJT items contribute 0–3; Self-concept and behavioral items contribute 1–5.
STEP 2  Convert each pillar's raw score to a percentage of its maximum possible score: Pillar % = (raw / max) × 100. Round to one decimal place.
STEP 3  Compute the Composite Score as the weighted average of pillar percentages: Composite = Σ (Pillar % × Pillar Weight) ÷ 100.
STEP 4  Map both pillar percentages and composite score to the five proficiency bands in section 4 to derive the descriptive level and recommended interventions.

proficiency_bands.title: ADVANCED
proficiency_bands.descriptor: Demonstrates sustained mastery. Ready for senior responsibility, mentoring others, and leading complex initiatives.

proficiency_bands.title: PROFICIENT
proficiency_bands.descriptor: Reliably effective in most situations. Benefits from stretch assignments and exposure to higher-complexity challenges.

proficiency_bands.title: COMPETENT
proficiency_bands.descriptor: Solid foundation with room to deepen. Coaching, applied practice, and structured feedback will accelerate development.

proficiency_bands.title: EMERGING
proficiency_bands.descriptor: Foundational understanding but inconsistent application. Needs targeted skill-building and supervised practice.

proficiency_bands.title: DEVELOPING
proficiency_bands.descriptor: Significant gap. Requires intensive learning, mentorship, and behavioral coaching before independent responsibility is appropriate.

item_types.name: Knowledge MCQ
item_types.hint: One correct answer. Choose the best response based on your knowledge.

item_types.name: Situational Judgment
item_types.hint: All options are plausible. Choose what you would most likely do in real life.

item_types.name: Self-Concept Inventory
item_types.hint: No right or wrong answer — answer honestly about yourself.

item_types.name: Reverse-Coded Self-Concept
item_types.hint: No right or wrong answer — answer honestly (some items are reverse-coded).

item_types.name: Behavioral Frequency
item_types.hint: Rate how often this applies to you (Never → Always).

item_types.name: Forced Choice
item_types.hint: Select the BEST and WORST option from the choices given.
